question.text: Research reports must disclose all of the following EXCEPT:

answer.text: Whether the analyst has financial interest in the subject company
Whether the entity received compensation from the subject company in past 12 months
Whether the analyst served as officer or director of the subject company

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 19 requires disclosure of financial interests, compensation received, IB relationships, and positions held — NOT personal medical history. **The Trap:** All other disclosures are mandatory under Regulation 19. Personal medical data is private and not relevant. **Cross-Exam Context:** Ch 14 NISM XV. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Disclosure transparency builds investor trust. Always read the disclosure section of research reports to identify potential conflicts.
